Demonstrate strong operational oversight capabilities, including daily activity monitoring to ensure tasks are completed on schedule and aligned with project goals. They should be adept at coordinating participants, resolving on-site challenges, and fostering discipline and motivation. Effective resource management is essential, with responsibility for overseeing tools, materials, and equipment to prevent waste and promote efficiency. A thorough understanding of occupational health and safety standards is required, along with the ability to enforce compliance with SEF and government regulations. The role demands meticulous documentation skills to maintain accurate attendance, payroll, performance records, and administrative paperwork. Strong communication and stakeholder engagement are critical, as the candidate will serve as a liaison between project managers, implementing partners, and community participants, while also managing conflict resolution to sustain a productive work environment. Quality assurance is key, including tracking performance against targets and contributing to impact reporting that reflects the social value of the work. Leadership qualities are essential, particularly in mentoring youth and women to develop workplace skills and civic responsibility. Finally, the candidate must be a creative problem-solver, capable of adapting to challenges such as absenteeism, resource shortages, and shifting priorities with agility and innovation.

Minimum requirement: National Senior Certificate (Matric). Skills and Competencies, Computer literacy: Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) and basic data entry systems.Organizational skills: Ability to manage files, schedules, and multiple administrative tasks efficiently. Attention to detail: Accuracy in capturing attendance, payroll data, and compliance documentation. Communication skills: Clear verbal and written communication for reporting and team coordination. Time management: Ability to meet deadlines and support multi-site operations.

Also to collaborate with caregivers and educators to intergrate play based learning into existing programs. You are also required to pitch up at work on time and fullfil all that is required of you within working hours.
